title: AML post cytotoxic therapy in a 56-year-old female with prior multiple myeloma

patient.chief_complaint: Progressive fatigue, pallor, exertional dyspnea, and spontaneous bruising with petechiae
patient.vital_signs: BP 124/78 mmHg, HR 99/min, T° 37.1 °C, RR 16/min, SpO₂ 96% on room air.

1.setting: Inpatient admission for newly detected blasts and cytopenias
1.capabilities: You are evaluating a hemodynamically stable patient with suspected acute hematologic malignancy in a hospital setting. You can perform a focused history and examination, obtain routine laboratory studies, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial inpatient assessment. Invasive diagnostic procedures and complex analyses require additional coordination but can be planned from this setting.

2.capabilities: The patient remains admitted for clarification of a suspected acute leukemia. You can organize bone marrow assessment, extended diagnostic workup, and baseline staging procedures. Interdisciplinary evaluations can be requested to exclude alternative primary malignancies or additional disease sites. Some specialized tests may not yet be available.

3.setting: Inpatient Ward / Pre-transplant Treatment Strategy Discussion
3.capabilities: You now have access to results from specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including molecular profiling, detailed classification of the leukemia, and a comprehensive treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with risk scores and transplant information to define prognosis and formulate a definitive treatment and transplant strategy.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics or background characteristics such as age, sex, performance status, comorbidities, social history, family history, or anthropometrics.
demographics.content:
Age: 56 years
Sex: Female
Performance status: ECOG 1
Comorbidities: Arterial hypertension, IgG kappa multiple myeloma (status post two autologous stem cell transplants), lenalidomide maintenance until June 2025, and recurrent cytopenia.
Social: Married, lives with her husband, two adult children. Never smoker, occasional wine consumption, no history of illicit drug use.
Family history: Father with ischemic heart disease; mother alive and affected by osteoporosis.
Height: 168 cm; Bodyweight: 72 kg.

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination findings, or current medication list.
history_physical.content: 2025-08-15: The patient presented with progressive fatigue, pallor, exertional dyspnea, and spontaneous bruising with petechiae. She reported a 2.5 kg unintentional weight loss over the past three months. No fever, night sweats, or chest pain were noted. Physical examination: The patient appeared pale, with scattered ecchymoses and petechiae on the extremities. Mild gingival hyperplasia was present, but no oral ulcers were observed. There was no palpable cervical or axillary lymphadenopathy. Abdominal examination revealed hepatomegaly (liver span 17 cm) and splenomegaly (14 cm). No focal neurological deficits were detected. Current medication: Ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, calcium 500 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IU once weekly, posaconazole 300 mg 1-0-0, and ASA 100 mg 1-0-0.

cbc.valid_request_if: Requests for a complete blood count, including hemoglobin, white blood cell count, platelet count, and differential.
cbc.content: CBC: Hb 7.9 g/dL, WBC 13.1 ×10⁹/L, Plt 18 ×10⁹/L; differential: 78% blasts, 10% monocytes, 8% neutrophils, 4% lymphocytes.

coagulation.valid_request_if: Requests for coagulation parameters such as INR or aPTT.

chemistry.valid_request_if: Requests for a chemistry or metabolic panel, including renal function, liver enzymes, LDH, and uric acid.
chemistry.content: Chemistry: Na 137 mmol/L, K 4.7 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, BUN 20 mg/dL, AST 72 U/L, ALT 64 U/L, bilirubin 1.2 mg/dL, LDH 880 U/L, uric acid 7.9 mg/dL.

immunology_myeloma.valid_request_if: Requests for immunoglobulin levels, free light chains, or immunofixation to assess myeloma status.
immunology_myeloma.content: Immunology: IgG 874 mg/dL, FLC-kappa 12 mg/L, FLC-lambda 16 mg/L. Immunofixation electrophoresis: negative.

ultrasound_abdomen.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound or sonography of the liver and spleen.
ultrasound_abdomen.content: Abdominal ultrasound: Demonstrated hepatosplenomegaly (liver 17 cm, spleen 14 cm) without ascites or detectable abdominal lymphadenopathy.

echocardiography.valid_request_if: Requests for transthoracic echocardiography or cardiac ultrasound findings.
echocardiography.content: Echocardiogram: Showed normal left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF 58%) and no significant valvular abnormalities.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow morphology, blast percentage, immunophenotyping, or fibrosis grading.
bone_marrow.content:
Morphology: Hypocellular bone marrow (20%) with approximately 80% infiltration by round to oval-shaped blasts showing open chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and deeply basophilic cytoplasm. Dysplastic megakaryocytes and granulopoiesis were noted. Residual hematopoiesis was markedly reduced with impaired erythropoiesis. No excess of plasma cells was seen. Flow cytometry: Blasts were positive for CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, and CD33, with aberrant expression of CD56. CD7 was negative. Plasma cells were polyclonal and accounted for <1% of total nucleated cells.
Immunohistochemistry confirmed hypocellularity of the marrow. Reticulin staining demonstrated fibrosis grade MF-2. Infiltration by CD34+ blasts (70%) with co-expression of CD117 and CD33 was confirmed. No atypical or monoclonal plasma cell population was identified.

cytogenetics.valid_request_if: Requests for conventional cytogenetic analysis or karyotype results.

extended_screening.valid_request_if: Requests for infection screening, serologic status, colonization screening, or general systemic evaluation.
extended_screening.content: Infection screen: CRP 2.6 mg/L, PCT 0.1 ng/mL. Blood cultures remained negative. CMV-IgG positive, HIV serology negative, EBV-IgG positive, IgM negative. Colonization: No MRSA, no ESBL-producing organisms; VRE positive in rectal swab.

gynecologic_assessment.valid_request_if: Requests for gynecologic evaluation, pelvic examination, cervical cytology, or transvaginal ultrasound findings.
gynecologic_assessment.content: Gynecologic assessment: Normal pelvic examination, negative cervical cytology, and transvaginal ultrasound without evidence of adnexal masses. Papanicolaou smear negative for intraepithelial lesion or malignancy.

ent_evaluation.valid_request_if: Requests for ENT evaluation or search for an upper airway infectious focus.
ent_evaluation.content: ENT evaluation: No infectious focus identified; normal otoscopy and rhinoscopy, no sinus pathology.

dental_evaluation.valid_request_if: Requests for dental or oral examination to exclude odontogenic sources of infection.
dental_evaluation.content: Dental examination: Dentition intact with no signs of periodontitis or abscess formation.

pet_ct.valid_request_if: Requests for whole-body PET-CT or staging imaging to search for extramedullary disease.
pet_ct.content: PET-CT (2025-08-20): No FDG-avid extramedullary lesions detected; hepatosplenomegaly without focal uptake.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for molecular profiling, next-generation sequencing, or mutation panel results of the leukemic blasts.
molecular_panel.content: CD138- fraction: Mutations in PTPN11 (VAF 11%), TP53 p.R248Q (VAF 28%); TP53 p.Y220C (VAF 12%). Negative for ASXL1, RUNX1, BCOR, CEBPA, DDX41, FLT3, IDH1/2, NPM1, SRSF2, STAG2, U2AF1, ZRSR2, KMT2A or MECOM rearrangements

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease characterization, leukemia-associated immunophenotype, or summary classification of the leukemia and myeloma status.
mrd_status.content: AML, post cytotoxic therapy (AML-pCT). PTPN11 and 2 TP53 mutations. LAIP: CD34+, CD117+, HLA-DR+, CD13+, and CD33+ and CD56(+). MM in stringent CR.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for detailed prior treatment history of multiple myeloma, prior transplants, and antecedent myeloid disease and therapy.
treatment_history.content:
Diagnosis (February 12, 2023): IgG kappa multiple myeloma. FISH: gain(1q21), del(13q), amp(1p), no del(17p). CT scan: multiple osteolytic lesions involving the thoracic and lumbar vertebrae, bilateral ribs, the pelvic bones, and the proximal femora. One lytic lesion in the left iliac crest was noted to be structurally unstable and at risk of pathological fracture; this lesion was treated with localized radiotherapy (20 Gy in 5 fractions) during cycle 1 at an external hospital. R-ISS stage I, following prior smoldering myeloma (diagnosed April 2022). Induction (Cycle 1, March 2023): VRD regimen — Bortezomib 1.3 mg/m² s.c. days 1, 4, 8, 11; Lenalidomide 25 mg PO days 1–21; Dexamethasone 40 mg PO weekly. Co-medication: ASA 100 mg daily. Patient was transferred to a tertiary care center for cycle 2 (April 2023): Dara-VRD with Daratumumab 16 mg/kg IV days 1, 8, 15, 22 plus VRD as above. Response: VGPR after Cycle 2. Cycles 3–5 (April–July 2023): Continued Dara-VRD at the same doses. Response: VGPR. Stem cell mobilization (June 15, 2023): Cyclophosphamide 2 g/m² IV days 1–2 (total 6,720 mg) + etoposide 100 mg/m² IV days 1–3 (168 mg per dose), followed by apheresis on June 29, 2023, yielding 7.9 × 10⁶ CD34⁺/kg stem cells.
Autologous SCT 1 (August 12, 2023): High-dose melphalan 100 mg/m² IV on days –3 and –2 (total 366 mg). Transplant with 2.0 × 10⁶ CD34⁺/kg. Complications: neutropenic fever treated with piperacillin/tazobactam 4.5 g q6h for 7 days. Engraftment on August 25, 2023. Response: VGPR (positive immunofixation). Autologous SCT 2 (November 8, 2023): High-dose melphalan 100 mg/m² IV on days –3 and –2 (366 mg total). Transplant with 2.22 × 10⁶ CD34⁺/kg. Complications: neutropenic fever treated with piperacillin/tazobactam 4.5 g q6h for 3 days, then switched to meropenem 1 g q8h for persistent fever. Acute kidney injury (creatinine peak 3.2 mg/dL) requiring temporary hemodialysis. ICU transfer for septic shock with invasive ventilation and vasopressor support (noradrenaline). Pulmonary fungal pneumonia treated with liposomal amphotericin B 5 mg/kg IV daily for 14 days.
Antibiotic therapy adjusted to renal function (meropenem 1 g q24h). Gross hematuria due to catheter-associated bleeding, requiring platelet transfusions. Engraftment on November 21, 2023. Response: CR (negative immunofixation, marrow negative). Maintenance therapy (January 2024 – June 2025): Lenalidomide 10 mg PO days 1–21 of each 28-day cycle. CT scan (February 2024): No new osteolytic lesions. September 2024: Worsening pancytopenia; bone marrow (October 2024) showed no myeloma. Histology revealed hypocellular marrow; no NGS performed. Counts improved after lenalidomide reduction to 5 mg/day. February 2025: Recurrent pancytopenia. CT scan (February 8, 2025): No new osteolytic lesions. Lenalidomide was held for 2 weeks, then restarted at 5 mg/day. Serological remission confirmed, consistent with ongoing CR. July 2025: Bone marrow revealed 17% myeloid blasts, consistent with therapy-related MDS. NGS revealed TP53 mutation.
July 12, 2025: Treatment was initiated with azacitidine 75 mg/m² s.c. or i.v. days 1–7 (q28) plus venetoclax 70 mg/day for 21 days (q28); lenalidomide was discontinued. Donor search was initiated; one matched unrelated donor (MUD) was identified but only available by October 2025 for donation. Both children were HLA-haploidentical but tested positive in cross match. The patient remains transfusion-dependent for RBCs and platelets every 3–4 days.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise description of the current leukemia status or relapse classification.
disease_status.content: AML-pCT, adverse-risk molecular and cytogenetic profile, refractory to aza/ven.

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for special considerations relevant to treatment planning, including bone marrow reserve, prior disease status, and patient preferences.
special_considerations.content: Patient with a history of multiple myeloma (status post ASCT x2, in ongoing CR), now presenting with AML-pCT. Despite comorbidities, she currently maintains good performance status (ECOG 1). Bone marrow reserve is markedly reduced; AML appears to be proliferative and refractory to HMA+venetoclax. She has strong family support and expresses a clear wish for disease-modifying treatment, declining palliative-only measures at this stage.

differentials: Recurrence of multiple myeloma or plasma cell leukemia

treatment_plan.action: Arrange urgent bone marrow biopsy and aspiration with cytogenetics and molecular genetics to confirm diagnosis and characterize the leukemia.
Discontinue ASA given severe thrombocytopenia with platelets 18 x10^9/L and active bleeding signs.
Provide transfusion support with red blood cells for symptomatic anemia and platelet transfusions as needed.
Initiate careful fluid supplementation and tumor lysis prophylaxis given elevated LDH and uric acid levels.
Obtain chest CT scan to evaluate dyspnea and exclude pulmonary pathology.
Consider careful cytoreduction if start of definitive therapy is delayed and blast count increases.

key_findings_quotes: Hb 7.9 g/dL with fatigue, pallor, exertional dyspnea indicating symptomatic anemia from bone marrow failure.
Plt 18 x10^9/L with spontaneous bruising with petechiae and scattered ecchymoses on the extremities indicating symptomatic thrombocytopenia.
2.5 kg unintentional weight loss over the past three months.
Gingival hyperplasia was present, characteristic of acute monocytic leukemia.
78% blasts confirming diagnosis of acute leukemia.
IgG kappa multiple myeloma (status post two autologous stem cell transplants), lenalidomide maintenance until June 2025 indicating cytotoxic therapy in medical history.
LDH 880 U/L, uric acid 7.9 mg/dL indicating high cell turnover and mild risk of tumor lysis.

round_2.working_diagnosis: AML with TP53 mutation and 17p deletion, post cytotoxic therapy, with myelodysplastic changes
differentials: Secondary AML evolving from therapy-related MDS
High-risk MDS with excess blasts and complex karyotype

treatment_plan.action: Assess patient's treatment goals given high-risk AML with complex karyotype and 17p deletion.
Initiate HLA typing and donor search given adverse-risk features requiring allogeneic stem cell transplantation.
Check availability of clinical studies for high-risk AML.
Present case in AML/HCT board for multidisciplinary treatment planning.
Continue intensive supportive care with transfusions and infection monitoring, noting VRE colonization and adapting empiric antibiotic therapy accordingly.

key_findings_quotes: 80% infiltration by round to oval-shaped blasts showing open chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and deeply basophilic cytoplasm confirms diagnosis of acute leukemia.
Flow cytometry: Blasts were positive for CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, and CD33, with aberrant expression of CD56 confirms myeloid phenotype.
No atypical or monoclonal plasma cell population was identified indicating no signs of MM recurrence.
47,XX,+1,del(17p13.1),-20,dup(1q) indicating 17p deletion and complex karyotype with high risk.
VRE positive in rectal swab requiring consideration for upcoming infections and adapted empiric antibiotic therapy.

round_3.working_diagnosis: AML-pCT (WHO 2022) after treatment of multiple myeloma with tandem ASCT and lenalidomide maintenance; ELN 2022 adverse risk (17p deletion, complex karyotype, TP53 mutation); refractory to azacitidine/venetoclax
differentials: Therapy-related AML (AML-pCT) after intensive myeloma treatment

treatment_plan.action: Stop azacitidine and venetoclax given proliferative disease refractory to HMA plus venetoclax.

Initiate 7+3 induction chemotherapy with cytarabine 100 mg/m^2/day continuous IV infusion days 1-7 plus daunorubicin 60 mg/m^2 IV days 1-3. CPX-351 is not favored due to TP53 mutation and higher myelotoxicity.
Initiate stem cell donation from the matched unrelated donor in preparation for allogeneic HSCT.
Plan allogeneic HSCT with fludarabine/treosulfan conditioning if complete remission is achieved after induction.
If refractory to induction, proceed with FLAMSA-RIC conditioning without prior salvage chemotherapy.
Continue antifungal prophylaxis given history of pulmonary fungal pneumonia during prior transplant.

key_findings_quotes: CD138- fraction: Mutations in PTPN11 (VAF 11%), TP53 p.R248Q (VAF 28%); TP53 p.Y220C (VAF 12%) with no alteration with therapeutic relevance.
July 2025: Bone marrow revealed 17% myeloid blasts, consistent with therapy-related MDS. NGS revealed TP53 mutation.
Treatment was initiated with azacitidine 75 mg/m^2 plus venetoclax indicating MDS has been treated with HMA and venetoclax but disease is now refractory.
One matched unrelated donor (MUD) was identified but only available by October 2025 enabling allogeneic HSCT.
Good performance status (ECOG 1) and expresses a clear wish for disease-modifying treatment, declining palliative-only measures.
Pulmonary fungal pneumonia treated with liposomal amphotericin B indicating increased risk for recurrence requiring antifungal prophylaxis.
